foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Calot triangle anatomy, cystic duct-CBD junction variants, and aberrant right hepatic artery are the key anatomical factors in iatrogenic bile duct injury

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy for gallstones is the most common setting for bile duct injury; understanding surgical indications provides clinical context

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Unrecognized or partially repaired bile duct injury leads to recurrent cholangitis from biliary stasis and stricture

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Post-injury stricture (Bismuth-Strasberg classification) is the most important long-term consequence requiring hepaticojejunostomy

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Bile duct injury presents with post-operative jaundice, bile leak, or biloma requiring urgent MRCP evaluation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Severe acute cholecystitis with frozen Calot triangle is a major risk factor for intraoperative bile duct injury

objectives: SU28.12: Describe the applied anatomy of biliary system. Describe the clinical
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Bile Duct Injury across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
